core_mechanism: an order-parameter phase transition in a many-body ensemble of locally-coupled units, where local interaction rules drive the collective into one of a few macroscopic ordered states, with transitions governed by a sharp critical threshold exhibiting discontinuity and algebraic (power-law) divergences — the same critical-phenomena / universality structure as equilibrium statistical mechanics
brief_reason: The abstract states that only a few well-defined macroscopic/collective states occur and that transitions between them follow a scenario involving discontinuity and algebraic divergences, drawing a deep analogy to equilibrium statistical physics — i.e. a symmetry-breaking critical phase transition.
